results.raw_output: Journalist Julia Angwin has filed a class action lawsuit against Grammarly's parent company, Superhuman, alleging the company violated privacy and publicity rights by using her name and likeness without permission in its AI "Expert Review" feature. The feature, which simulates feedback from figures like Stephen King and Kara Swisher, is available to subscribers paying $144 a year.
results.judge_reasoning: Summary accurately captures the lawsuit, parties, and feature details without hallucination. It is concise (2 sentences) and leads with the most newsworthy fact (the lawsuit). Includes specific names (Julia Angwin, Superhuman, Stephen King, Kara Swisher) and the subscription cost. Sticks strictly to facts with no added opinion.

results.raw_output: Researchers present ComFree-Sim, a GPU-parallelized analytical contact physics engine for robotics simulation. The engine uses complementarity-free modeling to achieve near-linear scaling and delivers 2-3 times higher throughput than MuJoCo Warp in dense contact scenes.
